html:
Exclusive to:  

The Hollow Stump is a naturally-spawning object from the Reign of Giants DLC, found only in a Deciduous Forest Biome. It is a large stump with a hole, with yarn hanging from its branches and a ball of yarn on the side. It is the home of a Catcoon.

The Hollow Stump can be destroyed with a Hammer to yield 2 Twigs, 2 Logs, and a chance of another random item, detailed below in the chart.

A Hollow Stump initially starts with 9 Catcoon lives. The life counter decreases every time a Catcoon from the stump is killed, and the Hollow Stump regenerates one Catcoon life every 120 seconds. If a Hollow Stump runs out of lives completely, the stump will go into an "empty" state. It will never spawn another Catcoon and will serve no purpose other than to be hammered down for its resources. Despite appearing to be mostly destroyed, a Hollow Stump in this state will drop the same number of items as a functional one.

Spawned Catcoons wander within 7.5 tiles of their Hollow Stump.

Drops

Chance Item
30%
20%
5%
2%

Trivia

  • Catcoons spawning a total of 9 times, as well as the cat having 9 unique "gifts", is a reference to the myth that cats have 9 lives.
  • Despite Catcoons being hostile towards Wortox, he still calls it "A home for friends".

Bugs

  • Sometimes Catcoons will attempt to enter a hammered Hollow Stump, resulting in them repeatedly hissing indefinitely.

Gallery

  • Hollow Stump icon on a Map.
  • A Hollow Stump as seen in a drawing from Art Stream #19.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

Exclusive to:
“It's a den in a stump.”
–Wilson
“I wouldn't burn it while someone lives there.”
–Willow
“Stump is suspicious.”
–Wolfgang
“I could curl up and die in there.”
–Wendy
“LIFE LOOP ITERATING”
–WX-78
“An excellent hollow for a den.”
–Wickerbottom
“Ugh, how many more lives do you have?”
–Woodie
“Vermin housing.”
–Maxwell
“Fascinating! A stump that regenerates cat-like creatures!”
–Wagstaff
“Cute meat lives there.”
–Wigfrid
“Trunk house.”
–Webber
“Looks like a nice place to crash.”
–Walani
“How many critters can fit in there?”
–Warly
“Whut lives in here. I'm stumped.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS KITTY WITHIN?”
–Wilba
“Anyone home?”
–Wormwood
“With all that yarn, there must be knitter inside.”
–Wheeler
“We all gotta sleep.”
–Winona
“A home for friends.”
–Wortox
“Something skritching inside.”
–Wurt
“I think something lives in there!”
–Walter
“It looks like something's been living in there for a while.”
–Wanda

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Gốc Cây Rỗng là một vật thể sinh sản tự nhiên từ DLC Reign of Giants, chỉ được tìm thấy trong Quần Xã Rừng Rụng Lá. Đó là một gốc cây lớn có lỗ, có sợi treo trên cành và một cuộn sợi ở bên cạnh. Đây là nhà của Catcoon.

The '''Hollow Stump''' is a naturally-spawning object from the ''[[Reign of Giants]]'' DLC, found only in a [[Deciduous Forest]] [[Biome]]. It is a large stump with a hole, with yarn hanging from its branches and a ball of yarn on the side. It is the home of a [[Catcoon]].

The Hollow Stump can be destroyed with a [[Hammer]] to yield 2 [[Twigs]], 2 [[Logs]], and a chance of another random item, detailed below in the chart.

A Hollow Stump initially starts with 9 Catcoon lives. The life counter decreases every time a Catcoon from the stump is killed, and the Hollow Stump regenerates one Catcoon life every 120 seconds. If a Hollow Stump runs out of lives completely, the stump will go into an "empty" state. It will never spawn another Catcoon and will serve no purpose other than to be hammered down for its resources. Despite appearing to be mostly destroyed, a Hollow Stump in this state will drop the same number of items as a functional one.

Spawned Catcoons wander within 7.5 tiles of their Hollow Stump.

== Drops ==

== {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* [[Catcoons]] spawning a total of 9 times, as well as the cat having 9 unique "gifts", is a reference to the myth that cats have 9 lives.
* Despite Catcoons being hostile towards Wortox, he still calls it "A home for friends".

== {{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs ==
* Sometimes Catcoons will attempt to enter a hammered Hollow Stump, resulting in them repeatedly hissing indefinitely.
